w20: Compute-optimized instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM for balanced, general-purpose enterprise computing and data processing workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC 7B13 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM for balanced workload performance.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a compute-optimized server with 16 AMD EPYC 7B13 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It delivers reliable performance for general-purpose computing and data-intensive tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-16 is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It utilizes 16 AMD EPYC 7B13 vCPUs, which are mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system includes 64 GB of RAM, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This configuration does not include dedicated GPU hardware or local storage. The CPU features 64 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets including AES, SHA-NI, and FMA for cryptographic and mathematical efficiency. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance and high memory throughput, with average multi-core results across standard compute metrics. The instance is priced at 0.4794 USD per hour. Given its hardware profile, this server is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, web serving, and various data processing tasks that benefit from balanced compute and memory resources without requiring specialized hardware acceleration.
